title: Return type of date_add is different from MySQL
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

```sql
drop table if exists test.test_interval;
create table test.test_interval(p int not null,o time not null,v int not null);
insert into test.test_interval (p, o, v) values (0, '1:2:3', 1);
select date_add(o, interval 1 day) from test_interval;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql> select date_add(o, interval 1 day) from test_interval;
Field   1:  `date_add(o, interval 1 day)`
Catalog:    `def`
Database:   ``
Table:      ``
Org_table:  ``
Type:       TIME
Collation:  binary (63)
Length:     10
Max_length: 8
Decimals:   0
Flags:      BINARY


+-----------------------------+
| date_add(o, interval 1 day) |
+-----------------------------+
| 25:02:03                    |
+-----------------------------+
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
mysql> select date_add(o, interval 1 day) from test_interval;
Field   1:  `date_add(o, interval 1 day)`
Catalog:    `def`
Database:   ``
Table:      ``
Org_table:  ``
Type:       DATETIME
Collation:  binary (63)
Length:     19
Max_length: 19
Decimals:   0
Flags:      BINARY 


+-----------------------------+
| date_add(o, interval 1 day) |
+-----------------------------+
| 2023-09-21 01:02:03         |
+-----------------------------+
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_fuzz(a INT NOT NULL, b TIME NOT NULL, c DATETIME DEFAULT NULL, d TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_fuzz (a, b, c, d) VALUES (1, '12:30:45', NULL, 'test');
SELECT datetime(b, '+1 day') AS new_time FROM test_fuzz WHERE a = 1;
SELECT datetime(b, '+1 hour') AS new_time_hour FROM test_fuzz WHERE d IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: date_add function returns incorrect data type
